user_query:
I'm a Utah voter (first time trying vote-by-mail) and I want a simple local, event-triggered automation that reacts to ballot status events and generates helpful drafts I can use. Please build a small tool that reads the provided inputs and writes the following outputs under the workspace.

Inputs (use these exactly):
- input/status_feed.jsonl: newline-delimited JSON records of ballot status events (some lines are intentionally malformed to simulate real-world hiccups).
- input/county_contacts.csv: mapping from county name to the clerk email address.
- input/raw_message.txt: a rough, anxious message I wrote to neighbors that needs a calmer rewrite.
- input/error_snippet.txt: console output from a naive run that failed on the malformed feed; analyze this.

Required behavior and outputs:
1) Event-triggered email drafting:
   - Read input/status_feed.jsonl and input/county_contacts.csv.
   - For each valid line where event is one of {"signature_mismatch", "rejected"}, create an email draft file at out/emails/{voter_id}_{event}.txt.
   - Each email draft must be plain text with this structure:
     To: <clerk_email>
     Subject: Ballot issue for voter {voter_id}: {event}
     (blank line)
     <body>
   - Body requirements (to keep this calm and useful):
     - Include the county name and the event timestamp in ISO format.
     - Include the exact phrase "please advise on next steps".
     - Be 180 words or fewer.
     - Do not contain any exclamation marks.

2) Rewrite my message into a concise, reassuring SMS template for neighbors:
   - Rewrite input/raw_message.txt for tone, clarity, and brevity.
   - Save as out/templates/vbm_reassurance_sms.txt.
   - Constraints for this template:
     - 480 characters or fewer.
     - Must include the phrases "Utah vote-by-mail" and "check your ballot status".
     - Include the placeholder {ballot_status_link} where a link would go.
     - Do not contain any exclamation marks.

3) Robust error handling and analysis:
   - Some lines in input/status_feed.jsonl are malformed. Your tool must skip malformed lines without crashing and append each skipped line to out/errors.log in the format:
     line <number>: <original_line>
   - Produce out/error_analysis.md that:
     - Has a first-line heading "Error analysis".
     - Explicitly identifies the malformed line numbers from the feed.
     - Quotes at least one exact error message line from input/error_snippet.txt.
     - Briefly states the likely causes (e.g., trailing comma, comment text) and how your tool handles them.

4) Summary for verification:
   - Write out/summary.json with these fields:
     - lines_total: total lines read from input/status_feed.jsonl.
     - lines_malformed: count of lines written to out/errors.log.
     - events_by_type: an object mapping each event type to the number of valid occurrences processed.
     - emails_created: number of files generated in out/emails/.

Notes and constraints:
- Do not use the Internet; work only with the provided inputs.
- All paths above are relative to the workspace. Create any needed folders (e.g., out/emails, out/templates).
- Make the rewriting and drafting steps essential parts of the automation (i.e., the script should perform them based on the events). Also, base your error handling on the issues suggested by input/error_snippet.txt and document your reasoning in out/error_analysis.md.

Deliverables: the working automation/script(s) you create and the generated outputs (out/emails/*.txt, out/templates/vbm_reassurance_sms.txt, out/errors.log, out/error_analysis.md, out/summary.json) produced by running against the provided inputs. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
